description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a distinctive, textured appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a dark coloration that contrasts with the lighter, speckled stone of the grooved surface. The block appears dense and heavy, likely made from granite or a similar durable stone. Its design suggests it is intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly to prevent vehicle access or guide pedestrian flow. The overall construction is robust, with no visible signs of damage or weathering.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with a grooved, striped top surface and smooth, dark sides. The grooves create a visually striking, textured pattern.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the side, showing the grooved top surface with the stripes running horizontally and the solid, dark side face.
description_view_1: This view highlights the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running vertically and the lighter stone surface contrasting with the dark sides.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, the grooved top is visible with horizontal stripes, and the solid side face is prominent.
description_view_3: The grooves are again visible running vertically, emphasizing the regularity and depth of the pattern on the top surface.
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
